title: From The Temporary To The Eternal Lessons From The Life Of The Apostle Paul
scriptureForWeek: Various scriptures related to Paul’s teachings on how power works in the life of a godly man as compared to power in the world.
weekTheme: Boys control; Men serve God will give us influence Have you heard this quote from Lord Acton before? “Power tends to corrupt and absolute power corrupts absolutely.” If you haven’t, you will as you get older and you will begin to see what power can do to a man. Power can turn kind men into tyrants, humble guys into jerks, and nice men into bullies. It’s amazing what a little power can do to mess a guy’s character up. A ton of what is wrong with the world today is due to men having used power badly. You may have heard of the #metoo movement. It’s a social movement of women speaking out about how men with power abused them and took advantage of them sexually. There is also the #churchtoo movement reporting on how men in churches used their religious power to take advantage of women. Politicians use their power to push their political agendas. Bullies use their physical power to push other guys around. Rich people can use their financial power to oppress the poor or crush other businesses. Power can do a lot of damage. But power can also do a lot of good.

days.title: The True Source Of Power
days.scripture: ROMANS 11:33-36 Oh, how great are God’s riches and wisdom and knowledge! How impossible it is for us to understand his decisions and his ways! For who can know the LORD’s thoughts? Who knows enough to give him advice? And who has given him so much that he needs to pay it back? For everything comes from him and exists by his power and is intended for his glory. All glory to him forever! Amen.
days.reflection: Have you ever wanted to watch a movie at home but couldn’t get the TV to work? You tried pushing the on and off button, tried resetting it, and nothing seemed to happen. Then finally, out of frustration, you check to see if it’s plugged in, and you realize someone unplugged it to plug the vacuum cleaner in and forgot to put the TV cord back in. So frustrating! Almost everything in our world today needs a source of power. Plants need the power of the sun to grow. Electronics need the power of electricity to function. Cars need engines, and our bodies need food. People need God's power to know Him and please Him. Without a source of power, we are like empty shells. In our modern world, people often try and find power in all kinds of ways other than relying on God. Guys your age can really fall into this trap. They can think they don’t need God to make it through life. They rely on their own power to get through school, meet girls, play sports, get to college, or handle the drama they deal with. But this is an illusion. Life will eventually overwhelm and break all men. It’s a true sign of manhood to humble yourself and acknowledge that you need God's power to get through life. God has unlimited power and lived to help us. He can help us internally. He can give us hope, comfort, courage, and love. He can also help us externally. He can answer prayer, intervene in situations, protect us, and open impossible doors. In today’s reading, Paul wanted to remind the Romans living in the middle of the Roman Empire that they had access to a source of power better than the world. Even though Caesar seemed like a god, and the Roman Empire was seemingly invincible, God was the power behind it and the power above it. Paul wanted the Romans to recognize God's power and not rely on Rome. You have a lot of choices about the power you will rely on as you grow into a man. You can rely on your own mental power and try to deal with the hard decisions of life. You can rely on your own physical power to make it through. You may be tempted to trust in the power of human love or even your family to fix things for you and sort stuff out. But a true man will rely on a true source of power; the God behind all things. Learn to rely on Him, and you will be amazed at what He can do on your behalf.
questions: If you could have any superpower you wanted, what would it be and why?
Where do you think most guys your age tend to look for their source of strength, comfort, hope, and joy? Do you think this is working for most people or not?
Ask the person you are doing this study with what their source of power is in their life. How did they come to identify, choose, and rely on that?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. “You are worthy, O Lord our God, to receive glory and honor and power. For you created all things, and they exist because you created what you pleased.”
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for His heart of love and His power to save. Ask Him for power in any areas you need help in.

days.title: What To Do With Power
days.scripture: PHILIPPIANS 2:3-11 Don’t be selfish; don’t try to impress others. Be humble, thinking of others as better than yourselves. Don’t look out only for your own interests, but take an interest in others, too. You must have the same attitude that Christ Jesus had. “Though he was God, he did not think of equality with God as something to cling to. Instead, he gave up his divine privileges; he took the humble position of a slave and was born as a human being. When he appeared in human form, he humbled himself in obedience to God and died a criminal’s death on a cross.” Therefore, God elevated him to the place of highest honor and gave him the name above all other names, that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, in heaven and on earth and under the earth, and every tongue declare that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
days.reflection: If you woke up one day and suddenly found that you possessed all of God's powers, what would you do with them? Would you solve world hunger? Would you eradicate evil? Would you fix the global economy? Would you save the environment? This is what happened to a man named Bruce in the movie Bruce Almighty. One day he woke up and realized God had given him His power. But as noble as his intentions were, rather than fixing the whole world, he mainly used the power to fix things for himself. As much as we like to think we would never do that, most men who do get power do exactly that. They often just pile up money for themselves, secure their position, and enjoy their privilege. To be a godly man is to learn what to do with power. The older you get and the more power you accumulate, deciding how to use your power will become the most important issue of your life. To be goldy is to choose to use your power for others. It’s to choose to sacrifice, care, empower, and fight for others. Honestly, this is what makes Jesus so extraordinary. Jesus actually was God almighty in the flesh. Jesus did have the power of God. But what did He do with His power? How did He use it? For Himself? No! Today’s scripture reading tells us what Jesus did with power. Look closely at these words. Though he was God, he did not think of equality with God as something to cling to. Jesus didn’t cling to His power. Jesus didn’t hoard His resources for Himself and leave us to solve our own problems. Instead, He gave up His divine privileges. Jesus could have stayed in the glory of heaven with His Father, enjoying the praise and worship of the whole angelic host. But He knew if He did that, we would all perish. He took the humble position of a slave and was born as a human being. He left His throne and became a man. He was born into humility and obscurity. He lived the life we should have lived, died the death we deserved, and rose again to give us power over sin, Satan, death, and hell. Jesus gave up his power to empower us. Jesus gave up His life so we could live. Jesus gave what He had for others. You may not wake up one day like Bruce Almighty to find God's power has become yours. But you will have chances every day to use the power you do have to serve others and put them first. Believe it or not, this may be the most God-like and powerful thing you actually do. Power is for serving, not selfishness. If you learn that, God will trust you with more power than you could ever accumulate on your own.
questions: Have you ever had somebody use their power to help you or bless you? What happened, and how did it help?
Have you ever used the power you have to help or bless others? How? How did this make you feel?

Ask the person you are doing this study with when they decided to start using their power for others instead of just themselves. What caused this to happen? How has this made a difference in their life?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. You see, we don’t go around preaching about ourselves. We preach that Jesus Christ is Lord, and we ourselves are your servants for Jesus’ sake.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank Jesus for His humility and love. Ask Him to give you a heart to use your power the way He did; for others.

days.title: Power To Fight
days.scripture: EPHESIANS 6:10-18 A final word: Be strong in the Lord and in his mighty power. Put on all of God’s armor so that you will be able to stand firm against all strategies of the devil. For we are not fighting against flesh-and-blood enemies, but against evil rulers and authorities of the unseen world, against mighty powers in this dark world, and against evil spirits in the heavenly places. Therefore, put on every piece of God’s armor so you will be able to resist the enemy in the time of evil. Then after the battle you will still be standing firm. Stand your ground, putting on the belt of truth and the body armor of God’s righteousness. For shoes, put on the peace that comes from the Good News so that you will be fully prepared. In addition to all of these, hold up the shield of faith to stop the fiery arrows of the devil. Put on salvation as your helmet, and take the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God. Pray in the Spirit at all times and on every occasion. Stay alert and be persistent in your prayers for all believers everywhere.
days.reflection: When guys your age think of following Jesus, they often think of having to do “churchy“ stuff. They think of reading the Bible, singing songs, and sitting through long services. They rarely view following Jesus as joining an army to fight in a war. You will remember that A.W. Tozer said, “What comes into our minds when we think about God is the most important thing about us.” If you have the wrong thoughts about God, you will live the wrong kind of life. Contrary to popular belief, as expressed in the secular media, following Jesus isn’t some passive, boring pursuit. It's signing up for the adventure of a lifetime in the middle of the greatest war in human history. Paul knew that converting people to Jesus meant converting them to fight. They had to rely on God's power and not their own if they were going to win. That’s why he wrote: Be strong in the Lord and in His mighty power... For we are not fighting against flesh-and-blood enemies, but against evil rulers and authorities of the unseen world, against mighty powers in this dark world, and against evil spirits in the heavenly places. You serve a powerful, conquering God. Psalm 24 puts it this way: Who is the King of glory? The LORD, strong and mighty; the LORD, invincible in battle. Who is the King of glory? The LORD of Heaven’s Armies— he is the King of glory. When the Psalmist asks who God is, the reply is a little shocking: A God who fights. A God who is invincible in battle. A God who is strong and mighty. This God has delegated some of His power to us. He has given us His power through the Holy Spirit that He put within us when we trusted in Him. God promised that “we would receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon us.” God doesn’t give us power so we can do cool spiritual tricks or become spiritual show-offs, but so that we can fight. He wants us to fight. Fight lies with truth. Fight hate with love. Fight evil with good. Fight oppression with freedom. Fight cynicism with faith. Whenever someone is given a weapon, they must learn how to use it. You must learn to fight with spiritual lessons and spiritual power. This will require training and practice to develop and deploy the power God has given you. The Bible isn’t just a book; it’s a sword. Faith isn’t just ideas about God; it’s a shield that protects you. Salvation isn’t just believing in God; it’s a helmet that stops you from getting knocked unconscious. Truth isn’t just spiritual thoughts; it’s a belt that keeps your armor together. Peace isn’t just a feeling; it’s a pair of powerful cleats that help you stand your ground. Worldly men use their power to fight their enemies to build their own kingdoms. Godly men use God's power to fight for God's kingdom. You aren’t just a Christian going to church events. You are a soldier. Grab your gear and get to war. The world is waiting.
questions: John Stuart Mill is the first person attributed to saying this: “All it takes for evil to triumph is for good men to do nothing.”
Where are some examples in our world today that evil is winning because men are passive? Are there any areas you can see in guys your age where evil is winning because they are doing nothing?
Does viewing discipleship as “enlisting as a soldier in a war to fight evil with God's power” change the way you see your faith? If so, how?

Ask the person you are doing this study with the share how they came to realize they were in a spiritual battle and had to rely on God's power. Are there any cool stories they want to share about how they have seen God's power overcome Satan?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. We are human, but we don’t wage war as humans do. We use God’s mighty weapons, not worldly weapons, to knock down the strongholds of human reasoning and to destroy false arguments. We destroy every proud obstacle that keeps people from knowing God. We capture their rebellious thoughts and teach them to obey Christ.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to pray for someone that you know is having a hard time right now. Pray against any plans that Satan may have to beat them down or discourage them. Ask for God's power to be released to help them and strengthen them.

days.title: Power To Suffer
days.scripture: 2 CORINTHIANS 11:21-33 But whatever they dare to boast about—I’m talking like a fool again—I dare to boast about it, too. Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I. Are they servants of Christ? I know I sound like a madman, but I have served him far more! I have worked harder, been put in prison more often, been whipped times without number, and faced death again and again. Five different times the Jewish leaders gave me thirty-nine lashes. Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I was shipwrecked. Once I spent a whole night and a day adrift at sea. I have traveled on many long journeys. I have faced danger from rivers and from robbers. I have faced danger from my own people, the Jews, as well as from the Gentiles. I have faced danger in the cities, in the deserts, and on the seas. And I have faced danger from men who claim to be believers but are not. I have worked hard and long, enduring many sleepless nights. I have been hungry and thirsty and have often gone without food. I have shivered in the cold, without enough clothing to keep me warm. Then, besides all this, I have the daily burden of my concern for all the churches. Who is weak without my feeling that weakness? Who is led astray, and I do not burn with anger? If I must boast, I would rather boast about the things that show how weak I am. God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, who is worthy of eternal praise, knows I am not lying. When I was in Damascus, the governor under King Aretas kept guards at the city gates to catch me. I had to be lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall to escape from him.
days.reflection: One of the great distortions people have in their minds about how a relationship with God works is that we never have to go through hard things. Boys can have simplistic thoughts about God that aren’t fully developed. They can think that the only factors that influence what happens in the world are them and God. Reality is reduced to “God and me.” Life is way more complicated than that. Your faith isn’t lived out in Eden; it’s lived out in the middle of a war zone. Satan hates God and His people and is seeking to destroy us. That means we will face spiritual resistance, sinful and broken systems, and human sin and rebellion. That means we are going to have to learn to suffer in order to serve other people. This is really how God's power works. The miracle isn’t that we avoid problems in the world. The miracle is that we have power to get through them. God gives us an overcoming spirit to handle whatever we face; not an escape pass to avoid all drama. God gives us power to get through impossible things. You may feel left out at school, but God will give you His power to deal with loneliness and rejection. You may not make the team, but God will give you power to deal with the disappointment. A girl may break your heart, but God will give you power to get through it. Your parents may go through a tough time and face financial challenges, people may get sick, family may die, and crazy things will happen, but God will give you power to get through it. You can see that in today's reading. Paul lived a wild life in light of eternity. He didn’t sit in a study reading books and twiddling his thumbs. Paul was dealing with unreal opposition and resistance. His life reads more like Lord of The Rings than a boring pastor. Paul was: Whipped. Imprisoned multiple times. Beaten with rods. Scourged with lashes. Shipwrecked and left at sea. Rejected, abandoned, and betrayed. Survived murder plots. Had to sneak out of cities at night so he wasn’t killed. Some of the churches he started turned their backs on him. YET... God gave him power to get through it all. Paul believed it was worth it in light of eternity. Suffering for its own sake is stupid; suffering in light of something glorious is worth it. Remember his perspective here? (2 Corinthians 4:16-18) That is why we never give up. Though our bodies are dying, our spirits are being renewed every day. For our present troubles are small and won’t last very long. Yet they produce for us a glory that vastly outweighs them and will last forever! So we don’t look at the troubles we can see now; rather, we fix our gaze on things that cannot be seen. For the things we see now will soon be gone, but the things we cannot see will last forever. God isn’t a genie we can use to grant us wishes and get us out of a scramble. God is present with us to help us get through the impossible. As you get older you will start having to deal with more and more resistance to your faith. Friends may reject you, teachers may mock you, and peers may make fun of you. All of this is very likely, but God will give you the power you need to handle this when the time comes. Jesus said that “In this world you will have many troubles.” Drama will come. Yet He also said, “Take heart, I have overcome the world.” Problems with power; that’s the miracle of how a man gets through life. There is power for your problems; ask for it!
questions: If you could wave a wand and remove one hard thing from your life right now, what would it be and why?
Has your family ever been through a really hard situation and experienced God's power to help them get through it? What was it, and how was God’s help made available?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share one of the first times they went through something hard and realized they were not on their own. What was it, and how did God show up?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. And let us run with endurance the race God has set before us. We do this by keeping our eyes on Jesus, the champion who initiates and perfects our faith. Because of the joy awaiting him, he endured the cross, disregarding its shame. Now he is seated in the place of honor beside God’s throne. Think of all the hostility he endured from sinful people; then you won’t become weary and give up. Hebrews 12:1-3 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to fill you with His power for anything you are struggling with or wrestling through right now. Take a moment to just sit quietly before God and let him fill you with His Spirit.

days.title: Trusted With Power
days.scripture: 1 CORINTHIANS 4:2-5 Now, a person who is put in charge as a manager must be faithful. As for me, it matters very little how I might be evaluated by you or by any human authority. I don’t even trust my own judgment on this point. My conscience is clear, but that doesn’t prove I’m right. It is the Lord himself who will examine me and decide. So don’t make judgments about anyone ahead of time— before the Lord returns. For he will bring our darkest secrets to light and will reveal our private motives. Then God will give to each one whatever praise is due.
days.reflection: John Eldredge says this: “The great problem of the earth and the great aim of the masculine journey boil down to this: when can you trust a man with power?” This really is at the heart of your journey into manhood. What will you do with power when you get it? You may not become a world-famous leader, but you will lead something and someone. Could be buddies on a team, your own family, other people you work with, or a small group at church. You will be given the ability to lead and influence the outcome of decisions and lives of others. A man has to learn to be trusted with power. Trust is such an important thing to build as you grow. Trust shows that you are responsible, diligent, and have character when no one else is around. Trust is both powerful and fragile. It's powerful because when you become a man, you are often given more power and responsibility. You get a larger say in what happens and what doesn’t. A trustworthy man will be given more freedom, money, gifts, and favor. He will enjoy better relationships with others and the respect of his friends. Being trusted is one of life’s richest rewards. But trust is also fragile. A lifetime of trust can be broken in a single moment. A man can have a long and celebrated career but then steal money, harass an employee, and get fired. His credibility in that workplace is gone forever. A man can have a happy marriage of thirty years, then cheat on his wife and destroy a lifetime of love. Trust can also be broken along the way. You can lie to your parents or friends about where you are going and destroy the trust you have built. You can cheat in your classes by taking stuff from the internet and passing it off as your own. You can hide things from your parents you know you shouldn’t be doing and lose trust when they find out. Trust is hard to get and easy to lose. Remember that. Can you be trusted with power? The story of the Bible is really just a story of what men did with power, and it’s filled with both failure and success. David was a man after God's own heart, but he used his power to kill a man and take his wife. Moses had power over the whole land, but he took his power into his own hands and killed an Egyptian. Joseph was given power over the land, yet he saved the lives of the nation through skillful leadership. He used his power for God. Jesus was God in the flesh, yet he used his power to heal, love, restore, fight for, and save others. How you use power has eternal consequences. You are accountable to God for what you do with your power. You will be remembered for how you used your power. You will receive eternal rewards for how you use your power. This week you have looked at using power in light of eternity. Don’t think about power like a boy; short-sighted and pursuing immediate gratification. Don’t use your power like godless men; building everything for themselves and taking advantage of others along the way. Use your power for good, for God, and to benefit those around you. Then you will leave a legacy that matters and have a heart full of joy. That’s as good as a man can get.
questions: Of all the powerful people in our world today (politicians, athletes, celebrities, tech founders, business leaders), who do you think does a good job of using their power well?

Can you think of a time when you built trust with your family? What was it? Can you think of a time when you lost trust? What was it? Which felt better and more rewarding? What did you learn from each situation?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share a time they used power well and a time they didn’t. What have they learned over the years about being trusted with power?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. For you have been called to live in freedom, my brothers and sisters. But don’t use your freedom to satisfy your sinful nature. Instead, use your freedom to serve one another in love. Galatians 5:13 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for the way that He has used His power. Creating the world, sending Jesus to save us, giving you the gift of life, and freedom from sin and death. Ask Him to give you His perspective on how to use power well.

extras: WEEK 5 MANSCHOOL HIT THE GYM MAN SCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: HIT THE GYM Men need to develop their strength. However, going to a gym can be intimidating when you are young. Figuring out how to increase strength and power can be confusing. This week, hit the gym. This can be at your place (if you have weights set up), a friend's place with a decent gym, or just a public park. Help him figure out how much strength he has. You may try maxing out pushups, timing sprints, or doing a squat max. If you get equipment, how much he can bench, squat, shoulder press, and curl? Give him an overview of each exercise and how it helps him increase physical strength and power. Try to get a decent sweat going and get him to find his limits. Give him a vision for growing in power in the days to come. Record where he is now and revisit it in a year to show him his growth and progress. Then go out after for some protein. A big steak of something that he loves. OPTION 2. WATCH: BRUCE ALMIGHTY A thought-provoking comedy that ends up making a good point. This is a Jim Carey classic and shows what to do and not do with power. You can check out the trailer here: https://youtu.be/fe-luzrqWSk
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
